Package org.mybatis.generator.api

Class FullyQualifiedTable.Builder

  • Constructor Details

    • Builder

      public Builder()

  • Method Details

    • withIntrospectedCatalog

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withIntrospectedCatalog(@Nullable String introspectedCatalog)
      Sets the actual catalog of the table as returned from DatabaseMetaData.

      This value should only be set if the user configured a catalog. Otherwise, the DatabaseMetaData is reporting some database default that we don't want in the generated code.

      Parameters:
      introspectedCatalog - the introspected catalog
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withIntrospectedSchema

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withIntrospectedSchema(@Nullable String introspectedSchema)
      Sets the actual schema of the table as returned from DatabaseMetaData.

      This value should only be set if the user configured a schema. Otherwise, the DatabaseMetaData is reporting some database default that we don't want in the generated code.

      Parameters:
      introspectedSchema - the introspected schema
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withIntrospectedTableName

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withIntrospectedTableName(String introspectedTableName)
      Sets the actual table name as returned from DatabaseMetaData.
      Parameters:
      introspectedTableName - the introspected table name
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withRuntimeCatalog

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withRuntimeCatalog(@Nullable String runtimeCatalog)
      Sets the runtime catalog.

      This is used to "rename" the catalog in the generated SQL. This is useful, for example, when generating code against one catalog that should run with a different catalog.

      Parameters:
      runtimeCatalog - the runtime catalog
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withRuntimeSchema

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withRuntimeSchema(@Nullable String runtimeSchema)
      Sets the runtime schema.

      This is used to "rename" the schema in the generated SQL. This is useful, for example, when generating code against one schema that should run with a different schema.

      Parameters:
      runtimeSchema - the runtime schema
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withRuntimeTableName

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withRuntimeTableName(@Nullable String runtimeTableName)
      Sets the runtime table name.

      This is used to "rename" the table in the generated SQL. This is useful, for example, when generating code to run with an Oracle synonym. The user would have to specify the actual table name and schema for generation, but would want to use the synonym name in the generated SQL

      Parameters:
      runtimeTableName - the runtime table name
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withDomainObjectName

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withDomainObjectName(@Nullable String domainObjectName)
      Set the configured domain object name for this table.

      If nothing is configured, we'll build the domain object named based on the tableName or runtimeTableName.

      Parameters:
      domainObjectName - the domain object name
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withAlias

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withAlias(@Nullable String alias)
      Sets a configured alias for the table. This alias will be added to the table name in the SQL.
      Parameters:
      alias - the alias
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withIgnoreQualifiersAtRuntime

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withIgnoreQualifiersAtRuntime(boolean ignoreQualifiersAtRuntime)
      If true, then the catalog and schema qualifiers will be ignored when composing fully qualified names in the generated SQL.

      This is used, for example, when the user needs to specify a specific schema for generating code but does not want the schema in the generated SQL

      Parameters:
      ignoreQualifiersAtRuntime - whether to ignore qualifiers at runtime
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withDelimitIdentifiers

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withDelimitIdentifiers(boolean delimitIdentifiers)
      If true, then the table identifiers will be delimited at runtime.

      The delimiter characters are obtained from the Context.

      Parameters:
      delimitIdentifiers - whether to delimit identifiers at runtime
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withDomainObjectRenamingRule

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withDomainObjectRenamingRule(@Nullable DomainObjectRenamingRule domainObjectRenamingRule)
      Sets a domain object renaming rule.

      This is ignored is a domain object name is configured.

      If a domain object name is not configured, we'll build the domain object named based on the tableName or runtimeTableName, and then we use the domain object renaming rule to generate the final domain object name.

      Parameters:
      domainObjectRenamingRule - the domain object renaming rule
      Returns:
      this builder

    • withContext

      public FullyQualifiedTable.Builder withContext(Context context)

    • build

      public FullyQualifiedTable build()
